coming back to this because that's how machines learn. That's how LLMs do their training, right?
They participate in a self-learning game where they're predicting, you know, the next word in
a sentence, but they already know the sentence because they've already digested the corpus,
and each time they do it, if it's wrong, they measure their loss, their gradient descent,
which points them in a direction in an n-dimensional space to go more right the next time,
and then they retest, okay? And so, I want us to try and embrace this concept in our class,
and whether it's in your practice exercises that you're doing in ScholarStack or whether it's with
this quiz, that's really, I want to kind of like always keep coming back to this concept,

what the concept of convergence means in the context of AI? Online, anyone?
It's the point at which further training yields marginal benefit, so it's basically when you stop
